_notes: This file is embedded in the axon CLI at build time and used as
the single source of truth for servo-model metadata, parameter
defaults, and bundled firmware file references.
TARGET BOOTLOADER: V1.3 (iProduct = 'USBBootloader V1.3').
The MK2 programmer (bootloader V1.4) adds parameters that are NOT
in the V1.3 set — specifically Left/Right Limit (distinct from
Servo Angle), Refresh Rate, and Power-On Sound. Those MK2-only
parameters are intentionally OMITTED from this catalog; they are
out of scope for v1.0 and tracked as a post-v1.0 epic.
The canonical V1.3 parameter list is sourced from the vendor's
archive docs (see source_docs above) and enumerates exactly nine
parameters: Servo Angle, Servo Neutral, Dampening Factor,
Sensitivity, PWM Power, Soft Start, Inversion, Loose PWM
Protection, and Overload Protection. This catalog implements as
many as we currently have a confident byte mapping for; the
remaining ones (Dampening Factor, Soft Start, Overload
Protection) error out with a clear message in the CLI and point
the user at docs/BYTE_MAPPING.md.
Model IDs (the `id` field) are the ASCII bytes at config offset
0x40..0x47 with trailing '*' as padding. Only the Axon Mini has
been confirmed empirically so far (SA33****); Max and Micro are
placeholders pending a physical read from those models.
IMPORTANT TERMINOLOGY: 'Sensitivity' (vendor name) is the PWM
dead-band (the microsecond window around neutral where the servo
doesn't move). 'Dampening Factor' (vendor name) is the D
coefficient in the internal position-control PID loop. They are
two DIFFERENT things and live at different config-block offsets.
Earlier revisions of this file incorrectly conflated them — that
has been corrected.

servo_angle._comment: BE u16 at 0x0A..0x0B = 0x0050 mirrored at 0x27..0x2C. Vendor docs say raw 0-255 maps to 0-355 deg, so raw 80 ≈ 111°. Unverified; needs A/B .svo diff.

servo_neutral._comment: byte 0x06 = 0x80; encoding stored = user_us + 0x80, so raw 128 → 0 µs offset (centered at 1500 µs).

sensitivity._comment: byte 0x0C = 0x50; encoding stored = (user_step + 1) * 16. Step 0 = 'ultra high' (1 µs deadband); step 4 is a mid-range deadband.

loose_pwm_protection._comment: Bits 0x60 of byte 0x25 set to 0x60 on the live Mini. Three modes exist (Release / Hold / Neutral); which numeric bit value maps to which mode is still unknown — needs a three-way .svo A/B diff to confirm.

pwm_power._comment: byte 0x11 = 0xDC mirrored at 0x12, 0x13, and 0x0F (= primary − 0x14). 220/255 ≈ 86%, which matches vendor's recommended default of 85% @ 6.0v. Verified by cross-reference to vendor docs; degree-of-fit is high but not yet A/B-diff-confirmed.

_Axon_Max_placeholder._TODO: Fill in once we can read a Max servo. Model ID unknown.
_Axon_Max_placeholder.name: Axon Max

_Axon_Micro_placeholder._TODO: Fill in once we can read a Micro servo. Model ID and firmware unknown.
_Axon_Micro_placeholder.name: Axon Micro

servo_angle.description: Operating travel of the servo. The vendor docs describe this as 'scaled from 0→255 to 0→355' — the raw value is a byte in 0..255 that maps proportionally to the servo's full documented angular range. Stored as a big-endian u16 at config offset 0x0A..0x0B and mirrored (same widget, same value) at 0x27..0x28, 0x29..0x2A, and 0x2B..0x2C; each mirror is an internal rate-limit register initialized from the user's Servo Angle setting.
servo_angle.deg_formula_note: Per vendor docs but not verified by A/B .svo diff yet. The catalog-level max_range_deg is per-model (355 for Mini). Needs confirmation that raw 255 really maps to 355°.

servo_neutral.description: Offsets the neutral position of the servo, measured in microseconds. The vendor UI calls this 'Neutral' (VCL event-handler symbol 'rztrNeutralChange' at 0x007a9888). Stored as a single byte at config offset 0x06 with encoding 'stored = user_us + 0x80', so raw 0x80 (128) is the factory-default 0 µs offset (i.e., the servo's neutral PWM is exactly 1500 µs). Significant adjustments can cause unintended side effects; use small changes only.

sensitivity.description: The PWM dead-band — the microsecond window around the neutral pulse where the servo doesn't move. Smaller dead-band = more sensitive to small command changes. The vendor docs specify 'Ultra high = 1us'. Stored as a single byte at config offset 0x0C with encoding 'stored = (user_step + 1) × 16', giving 15 discrete user-visible steps (user_step = 0..14). Step 0 is 'ultra high' (1 µs dead-band); higher steps give progressively larger dead-bands.
sensitivity._note: The mapping from user_step to microseconds beyond step 0 is unknown. The vendor docs only give the 'ultra high = 1us' anchor point. A .svo A/B diff with different Sensitivity settings would fill in the scale.

inversion.description: Reverses the direction of the servo. CCW (normal) is the factory default. Stored as bit 0x02 of the flags byte at config offset 0x25. A secondary 'dirty' bit at 0x01 is toggled each time the user changes the checkbox, but only bit 0x02 is the persistent setting.
inversion.confidence_note: Byte/bit location derived from the checkbox-toggle→persistent-bit pattern in FUN_00405518 lines 88-108. No literal 'Inversion' label string was found in data_printable_runs.txt; a .svo A/B diff of the vendor UI's Inversion checkbox would promote this to high confidence.

loose_pwm_protection.description: Servo behavior when the PWM signal is lost but power remains. The vendor docs describe three modes: 'Release: acts as if the servo wasn't powered', 'Hold: holds the last position it was set to', and 'Neutral: goes to the middle position'. Stored as two bits of the flags byte at config offset 0x25 (bits 0x60) — a 3-way enum. The specific numeric-value → mode mapping is not yet confirmed from the decomp alone and requires a three-way .svo A/B diff.
loose_pwm_protection.value_mapping_note: Byte/bit location is certain from FUN_00405518 lines 179-192. Which specific bit value (0x00, 0x40, or 0x60) corresponds to which vendor mode name is unknown. CLI should expose 'get' as a raw-bit annotation until A/B .svo diff resolves the mapping, and should reject 'set' attempts with a clear message.

pwm_power.description: Caps the maximum power output of the servo. Useful for use on current-limited devices. 0% = no power, 100% = max power. Stored as a single byte at config offset 0x11 and mirrored to 0x12, 0x13, and 0x0F (where 0x0F = primary − 0x14); the widget behind it (p1+0x370) writes all four in one pass. The 4-byte mirror pattern likely corresponds to the main cap plus the three progressive reductions used by the Overload Protection state machine — precise per-stage mapping requires the load-side decomp.
pwm_power.percent_formula_note: The live raw 0xDC (220) maps via this formula to ~86%, which matches the vendor's recommended default PWM Power of 85% for the Mini @ 6.0v — a good cross-check but not an A/B confirmation.

_not_yet_mapped._note: These are canonical V1.3 parameters (per the archive docs) that we cannot yet confidently map to specific bytes of the 95-byte config block. The CLI's 'axon get <name>' and 'axon set <name>' will return 'not yet mapped' errors for these names until the byte offsets are confirmed, and will point users at docs/BYTE_MAPPING.md. All parameters listed here are still round-trippable via 'axon read --svo' / 'axon write --from cfg.svo' since that operates on the whole 95-byte block.

dampening_factor.reason_blocked: Label string 'Damping Factor: ' exists at 0x007a734a and VCL symbol 'rztrDampingChange' at 0x007a98b4 confirm the UI widget exists, but we haven't found the widget's offset or the byte position in the current decomp. Likely lives in the 0x35..0x3A 'advanced mode' range or in FUN_00406248 (the secondary overlay). Needs FUN_00404b28 (the load-side function) decompiled to confirm.
dampening_factor.docs_description: Adjusts the D coefficient in the servo PID loop. NOT RECOMMENDED to adjust — it is tuned to work out of the box.

soft_start.reason_blocked: Not referenced in any currently-decompiled function. Probably one of the unmapped widgets (p1+0x3dc, p1+0x3e0, p1+0x3e4 in FUN_00406248). Needs more decomp.
soft_start.docs_description: Limits acceleration on startup; prevents sudden movement.

overload_protection.reason_blocked: Partially implicated in the PWM Power mirror pattern, but we can't distinguish 'main PWM Power' from 'stage-N overload reduction' without the load-side decomp. Bytes 0x14..0x18 (five monotonically-decreasing values 41, 33, 29, 25, 20 on the live Mini) and 0x19..0x1E (three BE-u16 values of 0x003C = 60) are strong candidates for 'per-stage power curve' and 'per-stage duration' respectively.
overload_protection.docs_description: Reduces the power output of the servo when stalled (to avoid burning out the motor). 3-stage progressive power reduction in Servo Mode; unavailable in CR Mode.

_mk2_only_parameters_omitted._note: The following parameters exist in the Axon MK2 programmer (bootloader V1.4) but NOT in the V1.3 target we're building against. They are intentionally omitted from this catalog. Adding MK2 support requires a separate reverse-engineering pass on the MK2 exe and access to a V1.4-era dongle and servo; tracked as a post-v1.0 epic.
_mk2_only_parameters_omitted.left_limit: Distinct from Servo Angle in MK2; one-sided motion bound.
_mk2_only_parameters_omitted.right_limit: Distinct from Servo Angle in MK2; other-sided motion bound.
